title: do not need timer feature in log tab
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

do not need timer feature in log tab. Delete it.

---

**Category:** `bug` `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User wants the timer feature removed from the Log tab.

What the user reported: The timer feature in the log tab is unwanted and should be deleted.

What component/area is affected: The Log tab UI — specifically any timer/stopwatch component rendered within the logging interface.

Expected behaviour: The Log tab should not show any timer or stopwatch UI. Users should still be able to log entries (feeds, diapers, sleep, etc.) without a timer widget.

Current behaviour: A timer feature is displayed in the Log tab, which the user finds unnecessary.

Edge cases to handle:
- If any logged data references timer durations, those historical entries should remain intact in storage
- If the timer state is stored anywhere (context, localStorage-equivalent), clean up references but do not delete historical data
- Ensure removing the timer does not break the layout or other log tab functionality

Acceptance criteria:
- Timer UI component is no longer visible in the Log tab
- All other Log tab functionality works as before
- No console errors or broken imports after removal
- Historical entries that used the timer retain their data

Testing:
- Add unit test verifying the Log tab renders without the timer component
- Add test ensuring existing log entries with duration data still display correctly
- Regression test that the Log tab still allows creating new entries after timer removal

title: Here is a strict, critical teardown of the UX and UI friction points visible ...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

Here is a strict, critical teardown of the UX and UI friction points visible in the app. When designing for exhausted, sleep-deprived parents operating the app one-handed in the dark, these issues will cause frustration.
1. The Floating Action Button (FAB) is Obstructing Core UI
 * Critical Overlap: The purple microphone button is poorly placed. In your scrolled-down Home screen, it completely overlaps the "All Stats" card and encroaches on the "Safety" icon in the bottom navigation bar. Users will inevitably trigger the microphone when trying to view their stats or navigate tabs.
 * Solution: The FAB needs a dedicated safe zone. You must add significant bottom padding to your scroll view so the lowest content clears the FAB, or tether the FAB to the bottom navigation bar itself.
2. Accessibility and Contrast Failures
 * Glassmorphism Legibility: On the Hero card (Feeds, Diapers, Sleep), the secondary text (e.g., "Formula · 4:29 PM", "Poop · 5:44 PM") is tiny and dark gray against a dark, blurred background. In low light, this is practically unreadable. You need a higher contrast ratio—either brighten the text significantly or increase the opacity of the card's background.
 * The "NOW" Badges: In the Guide section, the "NOW" indicator uses a muted yellow/orange text on a dark grayish-brown pill background. The contrast is too low to act as an effective attention-grabber.
 * Active States: In the Settings menu, the active "Ounces (oz)" button uses a bright cyan background with white text. White on cyan notoriously fails WCAG accessibility contrast checks and is hard on the eyes. Switch to dark text on the cyan background.
3. Touch Target and Ergonomic Risks
 * Accidental 911 Dials: The massive red "Emergency: Call 911" button at the top of the Safety tab is a massive liability for accidental taps—especially since parents are often scrolling frantically or handing the phone to a toddler. If there is no secondary confirmation modal ("Are you sure you want to call 911?"), you are guaranteeing misdials.
 * Log Editing Targets: In the Daily Log list, the "Edit" and "X" (Delete) icons are very small and placed immediately next to each other. A parent operating this one-handed with their thumb will frequently hit delete when trying to edit. Increase the touch target size to at least 44x44px and add more spacing between destructive and non-destructive actions.
4. Information Architecture & State Management
 * Hidden Content / Mystery Meat Navigation: The dots under the Hero card indicate horizontal scrolling, but there is no visual affordance indicating what is on the next card. Users might miss critical information because horizontal carousels have notoriously low interaction rates.
 * Unfinished Empty States: The dashboard aggressively defaults to "Baby" and "1 days" if data is missing or unconfigured. Seeing "Good evening Baby" feels robotic. The app should prompt for the name immediately during onboarding so the primary dashboard never looks incomplete.

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `ui` `content` `feeding` `diaper` `sleep`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
